text.title: mcp-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mcp-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: TypeScript "type": "module" requires Node.js >= 18; silent runtime errors on older Node.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin Node.js version in .nvmrc or engines field in package.json. Test with your minimum supported version.

failure-mode.description: FastMCP does not support streaming tool responses — use the official mcp Python SDK for streaming.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For streaming, use official mcp SDK. For non-streaming, FastMCP is simpler and faster to write.

failure-mode.description: If process.env.API_KEY is undefined in the TypeScript server, the server starts successfully but all tool calls fail at runtime — validate env at startup.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validate all env vars at server startup (before server.connect()), not inside tool handlers. Fail fast with a clear error message.

failure-mode.description: Large tool return values (>50KB) are passed verbatim into the agent's context window; they crowd out other context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always truncate large payloads before returning — text[:10000] or equivalent prevents context window bloat.

failure-mode.description: npx -y package@latest at Claude Code startup pulls the latest package version — a breaking upstream release can silently break all agents relying on that server.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin SDK versions in package.json and requirements.txt. Use "1.2.3" not "*". Pin packages in claude mcp add: npx my-mcp-server@1.2.3.

failure-mode.description: MCP Inspector requires a browser (port 5173) — use --cli flag or direct stdin testing in headless environments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use --cli mode for headless/CI environments. For development, browser GUI is convenient.

failure-mode.description: Python asyncio servers (async def tool handlers) cannot be tested with synchronous test harnesses; use asyncio.run() in test scripts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always use mcp.run() as the entry point for FastMCP servers. For official SDK, use asyncio.run(main()) in __main__.

failure-mode.description: An agent that builds a server and runs claude mcp add cannot use the new server until Claude Code restarts — the agent must finish and inform the user to restart.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plan multi-session workflows when server installation is part of the task. Document that agents must restart to use newly-installed servers.

failure-mode.description: MCP tool names are global within a registered server; if two registered servers expose a tool with the same name, Claude will invoke whichever it finds first — use descriptive, unique names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use namespace prefixes in tool names: {service}_{action}. Example: db_query, db_list_tables.

failure-mode.description: If a Python FastMCP server uses async def handlers but is launched with python server.py (not mcp.run()), it will start but all async calls silently return None.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always use mcp.run() as the entry point for FastMCP servers. For official SDK, use asyncio.run(main()) in __main__.

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot introspect what tools a server provides without calling list_tools — the MCP Inspector does this; in automated testing scripts, call list_tools first to verify server registration succeeded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In automated workflows, always call list_tools as the first validation step after registration.

failure-mode.description: Return values must be serializable to JSON text content — returning a Python object (dict, list) without json.dumps() will produce a [object Object] string in TypeScript or a type error in Python.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always call json.dumps() on Python objects before returning. In TypeScript, JSON.stringify() on complex objects.
